items.title: Broader encoding strategies
items.description: Hashing, frequency, binary, and embedding encodings address other cardinality and sparsity trade-offs; this topic owns one-hot, ordinal, and target-encoding pitfalls while the feature-engineering topic surveys the full toolbox.

items.title: Tree models need numeric columns
items.description: CART-style decision trees split on numeric thresholds; categoricals enter as one-hot columns (splittable at 0.5) or native categorical dtypes—raw strings cannot be consumed directly.

items.title: Linear regression and false ordering
items.description: OLS and logistic regression interpret encoded numbers as continuous; one-hot avoids false slopes on nominal data, while arbitrary label integers imply equal spacing between unrelated categories.

items.title: Leakage from misfit encoders
items.description: Fitting target statistics or category vocabularies using validation, test, or the same row's label leaks target information into features—the same failure mode as scaling on the full dataset before splitting.
